title: Stock and Quality Administrator (Full-Time)
description: **Company:** Caragh Nurseries **Location:** Caragh **Job Type:** Full\-time About the Role Caragh Nurseries is looking for an organised and detail\-oriented Stock and Quality Administrator to join our team. This role is ideal for someone who enjoys working both outdoors and in an administrative capacity. The successful candidate will play a key role in maintaining accurate stock levels across the nursery and production centre, ensuring our website inventory is kept up to date and reliable for customers. Working closely with the E\-Commerce Manager and Production Manager, you will be responsible for monitoring stock levels throughout the nursery, reporting inventory discrepancies, and coordinating incoming seasonal stock from our off\-site production nursery tunnels. Key Responsibilities * Walk the nursery daily to monitor and track stock levels * Maintain accurate inventory records for: * Plants * Pots * Garden furniture * Nursery products and related stock * Report stock updates to the E\-commerce Manager * Help ensure website stock quantities remain accurate and up to date * Liaise with the Production Manager regarding incoming seasonal stock from off\-site nursery tunnels and report discrepancies * Assist with stock counts and inventory organisation * Work closely with different departments to maintain efficient stock management systems * Ensure stock areas are organised and clearly managed * Monitor general stock quality and presentation throughout the nursery What We’re Looking For * Strong organisational skills and attention to detail * Tech savvy and proficient in Microsoft Excel * Comfortable working outdoors and walking the nursery regularly * Good communication and teamwork skills * Ability to work independently and use initiative * Basic computer skills and confidence using inventory or stock systems * Previous stock control, administration, retail, or horticultural experience is an advantage but not essential * Interest in horticulture or nursery operations is beneficial What We Offer * A varied role combining outdoor work and administration * Supportive and friendly working environment * Opportunity to work within a growing horticultural business * Training and development opportunities * Competitive pay based on experience Job Type: Full\-time Pay: From €28,000\.00 per year Benefits: * On\-site parking Language: * English (required) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

title: Supervising Pharmacist
description: **About Us** Chemist Warehouse Ireland is proud to be part of a globally recognised pharmacy retail group with strong family values at its core. While we are continuing to grow across Ireland, our wider Chemist Warehouse family has built an outstanding reputation across Australia and New Zealand over the past 45 years. We are passionate about delivering exceptional customer care, supporting our teams, and creating an engaging workplace where people can grow and succeed. **About the Role** We are currently seeking an experienced and motivated Supervising Pharmacist to lead and oversee the day\-to\-day operations of the dispensary while delivering exceptional patient care and professional pharmacy services. This is an exciting opportunity for a pharmacist who is passionate about clinical excellence, leadership, and team development within a fast\-paced and customer\-focused pharmacy environment. **Key Responsibilities** * Supervise and manage the safe, accurate, and efficient dispensing of prescriptions * Ensure compliance with all legislative, contractual, professional, and ethical standards * Lead and support dispensary staff, including pharmacists, technicians, OTC assistants, interns, and pharmacy students * Provide expert advice and guidance to patients and customers regarding medicines and healthcare services * Support and promote professional pharmacy services and healthcare initiatives * Oversee dispensary stock management and pharmacy claims processes * Ensure excellent customer service standards are maintained across the pharmacy * Mentor, train, and develop team members to support continuous improvement and professional growth * Build and maintain strong relationships with patients, healthcare professionals, suppliers, and the wider pharmacy team * Support the ongoing operational success and performance of the pharmacy **What We’re Looking For** * Registered Pharmacist with PSI registration * Previous experience in a Supervising Pharmacist or senior pharmacy leadership role * Strong clinical knowledge and professional integrity * Excellent leadership, communication, and interpersonal skills * Strong organisational and problem\-solving abilities * Customer\-focused with a patient\-first approach * Ability to lead and motivate teams within a fast\-paced environment * Passion for continuous learning, development, and service excellence **Why Join Us** * Opportunity to join a growing international pharmacy retail organisation * Supportive and collaborative working environment * Career progression and leadership development opportunities * Exposure to innovative pharmacy services and healthcare initiatives * Dynamic workplace focused on teamwork, development, and customer care **Work Pattern** Flexibility is required across weekday, weekend and Bank Holiday trading hours, including morning, mid and evening shifts. **Apply Now** If you are a dedicated pharmacy professional looking to take the next step in your leadership career, we would love to hear from you.

title: TFSM EHS Intern Engineer
description: **Job Details** Job Description: **This is a fixed\-term, 23\-month contract position.** The core focus of the role involves coordination, continuous improvement, and management of specific onsite EHS programs, with close collaboration with Safety, Industrial Hygiene, and Environmental Engineers. You will also support the overall EHS management system and ISO 45001/ISO 14001 standards. **Responsibilities** * Coordinate and support site Environmental Health and Safety (EHS) programs such as: + Hearing conservation + Working at heights + Personal protective equipment (PPE) * Conduct risk assessments to identify workplace hazards and recommend mitigation actions. * Support surveys and assessments associated with the Equipment Sign\-Off (ESO) program. * Act as a subject matter resource for assigned EHS programs. * Assist in accident and incident investigations, including root cause analysis and corrective actions. * Conduct regular audits to ensure compliance with regulatory requirements and internal EHS standards. * Support the implementation and maintenance of EHS management systems aligned with ISO 45001 and ISO 14001\. * Analyze EHS data, identify trends, and help define key focus areas for continuous improvement. * Collaborate closely with cross\-functional teams, including Safety, Industrial Hygiene, and Environmental Engineering. * Responsible for the development, co\-ordination, continuous improvement and management of site environmental programmes, in particular wastewater, and potentially support other programmes such as air emissions, noise, refrigerants, groundwater and management systems as required. Play a key role in ensuring compliance with the site’s Industrial Emissions Licence \- this includes review of compliance results/trends, Env Mgt System elements including awareness/training, documentation, Environmental Management Programme projects etc. **Qualifications** **Minimum Qualifications** * NFQ Level 8 qualification in a core EHS discipline, or * A third\-level qualification in science, chemical engineering, process engineering, or a related field, plus * Completion of a postgraduate diploma or MSc in an EHS discipline. **Preferred Qualifications** * 1–2 years of relevant industrial experience in a similar EHS role. * Strong communication skills, both written and verbal, with the ability to engage stakeholders on safety topics. * Ability to work effectively within a team environment with minimal supervision. * Self\-motivated and proactive, with a strong sense of initiative. * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills, with the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast\-paced environment. * Practical experience with EHS management systems and the ability to present and explain EHS data to both technical and non\-technical audiences. * Excellent presentation skills, with the ability to clearly and effectively communicate information. With our eyes on the future, we’re undergoing the greatest transformation in Intel’s history to shape tomorrow’s technology and lead the innovations that will improve the life of every person on the planet. If you thrive on solving challenges and are driven by curiosity, join us — and we can write our next chapters together. Discover your place in our world\-changing work. **Job Type** Student / Intern **Shift** Shift 1 (Ireland) Primary Location: Ireland, Leixlip Additional Locations: **Business Group** Intel Foundry strives to make every facet of semiconductor manufacturing state\-of\-the\-art while delighting our customers \-\- from delivering cutting\-edge silicon process and packaging technology leadership for the AI era, enabling our customers to design leadership products, global manufacturing scale and supply chain, through the continuous yield improvements to advanced packaging all the way to final test and assembly. We ensure our foundry customers' products receive our utmost focus in terms of service, technology enablement and capacity commitments. Employees in the Foundry Technology Manufacturing are part of a worldwide factory network that designs, develops, manufactures, and assembly/test packages the compute devices to improve the lives of every person on Earth. **Posting Statement** All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, religious creed, sex, national origin, ancestry, age, physical or mental disability, medical condition, genetic information, military and veteran status, marital status, pregnancy, gender, gender expression, gender identity, sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by local law, regulation, or ordinance. Position of Trust N/A **Work Model for this Role** This role will be eligible for our hybrid work model which allows employees to split their time between working on\-site at their assigned Intel site and off\-site. \* Job posting details (such as work model, location or time type) are subject to change.

title: Tax Associate
description: Our client, a well\-established medium\-sized accountancy practice in Dublin, is currently seeking a **Tax Associate** to join their growing tax department. The successful Tax Senior will be working as part of the Tax Compliance and Tax Advisory team on a diverse and interesting portfolio of clients which operate across a broad range of sectors. **Role** * Managing the compliance requirements of a diverse allocated list of Income Tax and Corporation Tax clients. * Providing compliance and advisory services for other tax heads including Capital Gains Tax, Capital Acquisition Tax, VAT and Relevant Contract Tax. * **Mentorship and development** from the Tax Partners \& Managers who will help you build confidence and broaden your skillset. * **Clear career path** with progression opportunities built into the role * **Flexible, hybrid working options** – enjoy a better work\-life balance without sacrificing career ambition **Requirements** * **The successful Tax Associate will be ACCA/ACA/CTA Qualified** (CTA is desirable but not essential). * **Minimum 1 year of PQE** within an Irish practice. * Strong technical knowledge and proven ability to identify opportunities for clients. * Display commitment, effort, motivation, and initiative in all business dealings. * Experience in handling Revenue aspect queries and tax registrations for individuals and companies. * Working towards or willing to complete CTA qualification is desirable **Benefits** * Competitive Salary: **€60,000 \- €70,000 DOE** * Friendly culture. * Clear progression pathway. * Hybrid work model. * 22 days annual leave * Paid CPD obligations. * Target based performance bonus. * Free parking * Social events * Wellness initiatives **Sound like a fit?** If you’re a Tax Senior and ready to build your future in a firm where you’ll be seen, supported, and given room to grow – get in touch with **Andrew Hendrickx** for a confidential, no\-pressure chat.

title: Tax Partner | North Dublin
description: **Tax Partner \- North Dublin** A progressive and growing professional services firm based in North Dublin is currently seeking to appoint a Tax Partner to join its leadership team. This represents an excellent opportunity for an experienced tax professional looking to step into a senior strategic role within an established and highly regarded practice environment. The firm has an existing and experienced tax team in place, servicing a diverse portfolio of domestic and international clients across a broad range of sectors. The role offers the opportunity to work across multiple tax heads while also providing genuine scope to influence the future direction and growth of the tax practice. A growth share opportunity is also on offer for the successful candidate. **The Opportunity** The successful candidate will work closely with the wider leadership team and existing tax professionals, advising owner\-managed businesses, SMEs, international groups, high\-net\-worth individuals and entrepreneurial clients on a broad range of tax matters. This role would suit an ambitious Director ready to step into Partnership or an existing Partner seeking a new environment with strong growth potential, quality clients and a collaborative culture. Key Responsibilities * Lead and manage a diverse tax portfolio across corporate, income, CGT, VAT and succession planning matters * Provide high\-quality advisory and consultancy services to a broad client base * Support and mentor an established tax team, fostering continued development and technical excellence * Build and maintain strong long\-term client relationships * Work closely with Partners across audit, accounting and advisory functions on cross\-disciplinary assignments * Identify business development opportunities and contribute to the continued growth of the tax division * Provide commercially focused tax solutions tailored to client needs * Stay abreast of Irish tax legislation and market developments **Candidate Profile** * CTA and/or ACA/ACCA qualified * Significant tax experience gained within a professional services environment * Strong technical tax capability across multiple tax heads * Proven leadership and client management experience * Commercially minded with strong interpersonal and relationship\-building skills * Business development capability and an interest in contributing strategically to the growth of the firm * Ambitious, collaborative and solutions\-focused The firm offers a strong platform, excellent support infrastructure, an established client base and genuine long\-term progression within a collaborative and entrepreneurial environment, together with the opportunity to participate in the future growth and success of the practice. All applications will be treated in the strictest confidence. Patrick O’Rourke is a Professional Services Recruitment Specialist, experienced in placing candidates from entry to Partner level. For a confidential discussion around opportunities in the market, please contact me directly \- T \- 087 190 2963\.

title: Technical Support Engineer
description: **Who is Tenable?** Tenable® is the Exposure Management company. 44,000 organizations around the globe rely on Tenable to understand and reduce cyber risk. Our global employees support 65 percent of the Fortune 500, 45 percent of the Global 2000, and large government agencies. Come be part of our journey! **What makes Tenable such a great place to work?** Ask a member of our team and they’ll answer, “Our people!” We work together to build and innovate best\-in\-class cybersecurity solutions for our customers; all while creating a culture of belonging, respect, and excellence where we can be our best selves. When you’re part of our \#OneTenable team, you can expect to partner with some of the most talented and passionate people in the industry, and have the support and resources you need to do work that truly matters. We deliver results that exceed expectations and we win together! **Your Role** Join our Technical Support Team at Tenable where you’ll play a pivotal role in bridging the gap between our products and customers. Solve real\-world technical challenges by offering exceptional support for cutting\-edge vulnerability assessment and compliance auditing software. Your expertise will shine in complex, multi\-layered customer environments. Our suite of products, detailed at https://www.tenable.com/products, offers a diverse range of opportunities. From assisting customers with their first Nessus scanner to guiding them through intricate log correlation and real\-time vulnerability analysis, you'll contribute to mitigating cyber risks and closing the Cyber Exposure gap. Join us in delivering top\-notch support that makes a difference! **Your Opportunity** * Serves as the primary technical liaison between the customer and Tenable internal groups (Product Management, Sales, R\&D, etc) for technical issues. * Analyse and clarify customer technical inquiries. * Analysing vulnerability scan results, system audits, and log events. * Recreating customer software issues in a lab environment. * Harness AI\-driven technologies to amplify your impact, automate routine tasks, and focus on solving complex technical challenges. * Maintaining in\-depth knowledge of Tenable products and information security best\-practices. * Create and publish solution knowledge for re\-use by customers and Tenable employees. **What You'll Need** * Bachelor’s degree in IT or Computer Science (or equivalent experience). * 2\+ years professional experience with networking (TCP/IP), OS administration(Linux/Windows/MacOS), patch deployment, and system configuration. * Experience providing technical support to enterprise clients, troubleshooting complex issues in production or customer environments; network security experience a plus. * Familiarity with Artificial Intelligence (AI) as Tenable continues to build an AI\-augmented support model to accelerate case resolutions and to provide an exceptional customer/TSE experience. * Knowledge of computer, network, and application security; familiarity with Nessus or similar tools is a bonus. * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills, with the ability to learn quickly. * Excellent written and verbal communication; customer\-focused mindset. * Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast\-paced environment to help meet business and client needs. * Monthly availability to work weekends (8am\-4:30pm) and Holidays is also required. **And Ideally** * Using Nessus and/or other vulnerability management or cybersecurity tools, either professionally or in an academic setting. * Experience with ticketing systems (JIRA, Salesforce, Zendesk etc). * Log analysis using a SEIM product (Splunk, ElasticSearch, etc). * Database technologies and SQL knowledge (Oracle, MySQL, MSSQL, etc). * Cloud/Virtualization technologies (AWS, Azure, Docker, Kubernetes, etc). * Fundamental understanding of programming languages. * Experience with or understanding of Certificates and CA’s. * Basic understanding of reviewing PCAPs with tools such as Wireshark. * Bilingual candidates are desirable but not required (Arabic/French/German/Spanish/Italian). *We’re committed to promoting Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) at Tenable \- through all equal employment opportunity laws and regulations at the international, federal, state and local levels. If you need a reasonable accommodation due to a disability during the application or recruiting process, please contact* *Recruiting@Tenable.com* *for further assistance.* **Tenable Data Consent Statement** Tenable is committed to protecting the privacy and security of your personal data. This Notice describes how we collect and use your personal data during and after your working relationship with us, in accordance with the General Data Protection Regulation (“GDPR”). Please click here to review. For California Residents: The California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) requires that Tenable advise you of certain rights related to the collection of your private information. Please click here to review.

title: Technical Support Professional – IBM Sovereign Core
description: **Introduction** At IBM Software, we transform client challenges into solutions. Building the world’s leading AI\-powered, cloud\-native products that shape the future of business and society. Our legacy of innovation creates endless opportunities for IBMers to learn, grow, and make an impact on a global scale. Working in Software means joining a team fueled by curiosity and collaboration. You’ll work with diverse technologies, partners, and industries to design, develop, and deliver solutions that power digital transformation. With a culture that values innovation, growth, and continuous learning, IBM Software places you at the heart of IBM’s product and technology landscape. Here, you’ll have the tools and opportunities to advance your career while creating software that changes the world. **Your Role And Responsibilities** Role Overview The IBM Sovereign Core Technical Support Professional plays a critical role in supporting secure, compliant, and customer‑operated environments built on IBM’s Sovereign Core software stack. In this position, you ensure the reliability, resilience, and smooth operation of mission‑critical workloads running within strict sovereign boundaries. You will troubleshoot complex technical issues, support deployments in regulated and air‑gapped environments, and act as a trusted advisor for customers adopting IBM Sovereign Core. **Responsibilities** * Manage support cases end‑to‑end—from triage through resolution—diagnosing and resolving issues across Sovereign Core components, including the control plane, embedded security services, identity systems, and AI/ML inference runtimes running on Red Hat OpenShift. * Support Sovereign Core deployments across on‑premises, air‑gapped, regulated environments, ensuring all work adheres to sovereignty, data‑residency, and operational‑control requirements. * Provide timely communication within a 24x7x365 support framework, coordinating incident response and mitigating issues affecting mission‑critical workloads. * Act as a customer advocate, identifying and escalating pain points, providing guidance, and contributing to improvements that enhance IBM Sovereign Core reliability, resiliency, and sovereign assurance. As part of maintaining continuous service coverage, the role includes participation in weekend standby and shift rotations for handling out‑of‑hours incidents. Due to the nature of this role and the associated client requirements, which include working with EU‑based customers in a sovereign environment, applicants must, by the job start date, lawfully reside in, be legally authorized to work in, and perform the role exclusively within the EU country of employment. **Preferred Education** Master's Degree **Required Technical And Professional Expertise** Technical Skills * Hands\-on experience with Red Hat OpenShift or Kubernetes platform administration. * Familiarity with compliance‑driven operations (e.g., audit trails, policy enforcement, governance frameworks). * Understanding of AI/ML inference pipelines, GPU scheduling, and model deployment workflows on container platforms. * Experience with encryption, secret management, and confidential computing concepts. Soft Skills * Demonstrate a growth mindset and ability to learn new technologies in a fast‑paced environment. * Strong communicator who can translate complex technical topics for a range of audiences and represent customer needs. * Problem\-solving mindset with ability to navigate technical and business constraints * Collaborative mindset with cross\-functional teams across engineering, operations, and compliance

title: Test Analyst
description: We are seeking an experienced IT Test Analyst to join a dynamic technology team responsible for delivering high\-quality digital solutions. The successful candidate will play a key role in ensuring that new systems, enhancements, and integrations meet defined business and technical standards. This is a hands\-on testing role requiring strong analytical ability, a structured approach to quality assurance, and a commitment to excellence. **Role Purpose** The IT Test Analyst will be responsible for planning, executing, and coordinating all phases of software testing across multiple projects. This includes developing test strategies, designing test cases, managing test environments, and collaborating closely with development and business teams to ensure successful delivery. **Core Responsibilities** * Analyse business and technical requirements to understand system functionality and testing needs. * Design and maintain comprehensive test plans, scripts, and scenarios to validate functionality, usability, performance, and security. * Conduct manual and automated testing activities to verify system behaviour and data integrity. * Manage test environments, datasets, and configurations to support accurate and repeatable test execution. * Identify, log, and track software defects through to resolution using appropriate tools and processes. * Provide clear, concise reporting on test outcomes, risks, and quality metrics to stakeholders. * Collaborate closely with developers, analysts, and project managers to resolve issues and enhance solution quality. * Support the continuous improvement of testing processes, tools, and documentation standards. **Experience \& Qualifications** * Minimum 3 years’ experience in a software testing or QA role within a technology\-focused environment. * Relevant third\-level qualification in Information Technology, Computer Science, or a related field. * Proven experience testing web, cloud, or enterprise applications. * Strong knowledge of software testing principles, tools, and methodologies. * Understanding of the software development lifecycle (SDLC) and common delivery approaches such as Agile or Waterfall. * Experience with defect management and test management tools (e.g., JIRA, Azure DevOps, TestRail). * Ability to create and maintain traceability between requirements, test cases, and results. * Excellent analytical, documentation, and communication skills. * Strong attention to detail and a commitment to producing high\-quality work. * Ability to work independently and contribute effectively within a team setting. **Desirable Attributes** * Professional certifications in software testing or quality assurance (e.g., ISTQB Foundation Level or equivalent). * Experience with test automation frameworks or scripting languages. * Exposure to API, integration, or performance testing. * Familiarity with DevOps and CI/CD environments. * Demonstrated initiative, curiosity, and a proactive approach to learning new tools and technologies. **Personal Qualities** * A structured thinker who enjoys problem\-solving and continuous improvement. * Excellent communicator who can translate technical concepts for non\-technical stakeholders. * Reliable, accountable, and focused on delivering results. * Adaptable and comfortable working in a fast\-paced environment with changing priorities.

title: Trading Operations Associate
description: PENN Entertainment, Inc. is North America’s leading provider of integrated entertainment, sports content, and casino gaming experiences. From casinos and racetracks to online gaming, sports betting and entertainment content, we deliver the experiences people want, how and where they want them. We’re always on the lookout for those who are passionate about creating and delivering cutting\-edge online gaming and sports media products. Whether it’s through Hollywood Casino, theScore Bet Sportsbook, or theScore media app, we’re excited to push the boundaries of what’s possible. These state\-of\-the\-art platforms are powered by proprietary in\-house technology, a key component of PENN’s omnichannel gaming and entertainment strategy. When you join PENN Entertainment’s digital team, you’ll not only work on these cutting\-edge platforms through theScore and PENN Interactive, but you’ll also be part of a company that truly cares about your career growth. We’re committed to supporting you as you expand your skills and explore new opportunities. With locations throughout North America, you can build a future at PENN Entertainment wherever you are. If you want to challenge conventions in gaming, media and entertainment, we want to talk to you. **About The Role And Team** Our Risk and Trading Operations team is available 24/7 to assist the various functions in the department (Trading/Marketing/Customer Support/etc.) in troubleshooting and escalating issues on all our operational sports betting platforms.This team is critical in ensuring the Risk \& Trading department consistently delivers one of its core goals of “operational excellence”. **About The Work** * Assist Traders with identifying, documenting, and escalating issues within the team * Support verification, adjustment \& creation of Sport \& Competition setups across environments * Help test and monitor app features to ensure proper functionality and navigation * Contribute to Compliance checks, market submissions, and post\-release testing * Communicate with data providers and support ticketing * Daily review of betting content * Monitoring pre\-game and in\-play markets * Cross\-functional assignments with Compliance, Marketing \& Product * Assist Customer Experience with issue resolution * Other duties as assigned **About You** * Post\-secondary degree, or equivalent work experience * Must be able to work weekends, evenings and holidays * Extensive sports knowledge, particularly with regards to the four major professional sports leagues; esports knowledge considered an asset * Sports betting knowledge \& experience would provide added value * Clear and effective communication skills * Must be computer savvy * Experience using iPhone and Android devices **What We Offer** * Competitive compensation package * Comprehensive Benefits package * Fun, relaxed work environment * Education and conference reimbursements *Penn Interactive is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace. We will consider all qualified applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, veteran status, genetic information, or any other basis protected by applicable law.Base pay is one part of the Total Rewards that Penn Interactive provides to compensate and recognize employees for their work. Most sales positions are eligible for a Commission under the terms of an applicable plan, while most non\-sales positions are eligible for a Bonus. Additionally, Penn Interactive provides best\-in\-class benefits to eligible employees. We believe that benefits should connect you to the support you need when it matters most, and should help you care for those who matter most. That’s why we provide an array of options, expert guidance and always\-on tools, that are personalized to meet the needs of your reality – to help support you physically, financially and emotionally through the big milestones and in your everyday life.*

title: Trainee Designer
description: **Trainee Designer \-** **Menswear** Because your ideas are our style. Design, our way. **Design at Primark** Our design specialists are at the heart of crafting captivating brand experiences. Immerse yourself in the world of visual storytelling, where your creativity will shape the aesthetics of our brand. Collaborate with a team of skilled designers and bring your artistic talent to a global stage. As a Trainee Designer, you will support the Designer and Buying teams in researching and designing a commercial range, including trend, colour, shape direction You will be responsible for staying ahead of trends, continually research new ideas. **What You’ll Do as a Trainee Designer** In your role, you’ll collaborate with different people across a range of skillsets. Here’s a flavour of your day\-to\-day: * Conduct daily trend research across multiple platforms and share insights with the womenswear teams. * Translate latest trends into commercially viable designs aligned with Primark’s brand handwriting. * Collaborate with the design team to create strong, original graphics and prints. * Support range development by considering garment shapes, fabrics, and seasonal colour direction. * Manage design updates, track amendments, and ensure timely communication across teams. * Prepare design files, presentations, and handle colour/trend/product\-related admin tasks. **What You’ll Get** People are at the heart of what we do here, so it’s essential we provide you with the right environment to perform at your very best. Let’s talk lifestyle: 27 days of leave, plus bank holidays and if you want, you can buy 5 more. Flexible Working, opportunity for an early Friday finish and a subsidised cafeteria. Because Primark is all about tailoring to you, we offer Tax Saver Tickets, fitness centre, and a subsidised cafeteria. **What You’ll Bring** We want every person in our organisation to feel like they’re making a key contribution. Here are some of the experiences and skills you’ll need for the role: * Bring a strong creative mindset with innovative thinking and a passionate, hard\-working approach. * Demonstrate a high\-quality portfolio showcasing original graphic and fashion design work. * Communicate ideas confidently and proactively to design and buying teams. * Maintain strong attention to detail while delivering commercially relevant designs. * Thrive in fast\-paced environments with the ability to multitask, adapt and meet tight deadlines. * Utilize Adobe Creative Cloud (Illustrator, Photoshop, InDesign) and Excel, with skills in both hand drawing and digital design. Does this sound like you? Great, because we can’t wait to see what you’ll bring. You’ll be supported within a team of equally capable people, celebrating who you are and aiding you reach your potential. At Primark, we’re excited about our future \- and we’re excited to develop yours. **About Primark** At Primark, people matter. They’re the beating heart of our business and the reason we’ve grown from our first store in Dublin in 1969 to a £9bn\+ turnover business and over 80,000 colleagues and over 440 stores in 17 countries today. Our values run through everything we do. In essence, we're Caring and always strive to put people first. We're also Dynamic, bravely pushing the boundaries to stay ahead. And finally, we succeed Together. Our fashion isn’t one\-size\-fits\-all and neither is our culture. Primark promotes equal employment opportunity, we strive to create an inclusive workplace where people can be themselves, access opportunities and thrive together. \#LI\-Hybrid **REQ ID:** JR\-7954

title: Trainee Welder / Fitter
description: **Niall Russell Engineering Limited** is a well\-established, professional engineering company known for our high standards, innovative solutions, and supportive work environment. Based in Tullyallen, we work with a diverse client base across multiple sectors, delivering high\-quality fabrication and engineering services. We are now seeking a Trainee Welder/Fitter to join our team. This is an excellent opportunity for someone eager to develop a career in welding and metal fabrication within a structured and progressive company. **Key Responsibilities** : As a Trainee Welder/Fitter, your duties will include: * Assisting in the fabrication and fitting of metal components * Learning to read and interpret technical drawings and blueprints * Supporting experienced welders and fitters on various projects * Using hand tools, grinders, and cutting equipment * Maintaining a clean and safe working environment * Committing to training and development plans * Working both in the workshop and on\-site (as required) **What We’re Looking For** **Essential:** * A genuine interest in welding, metalwork, or mechanical fitting * Willingness to learn and follow instruction * Good work ethic and timekeeping * Strong attention to detail and safety awareness * Ability to work as part of a team * Physically fit and capable of manual handling **Desirable (but not essential):** Any prior experience with tools, machinery, or fabrication (e.g. from school, hobbies, or previous jobs) Relevant qualification or course in engineering, metalwork, or welding **Training \& Career Development** We are committed to investing in your future. You will receive: * Hands\-on training from experienced welders and engineers * The opportunity to work towards certified welding qualifications * Ongoing mentoring and career guidance * Clear progression routes to become a fully qualified Welder/Fitter * Opportunities to gain experience in different engineering areas **Compensation \& Benefits** * Competitive entry\-level hourly rate (increases with progress and skills) * Full\-time, permanent role with job security * Overtime opportunities * PPE and tools provided * Continuous professional development * Friendly and supportive team environment * Future opportunity to specialise in fabrication, fitting, or site work Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Work Location: In person

title: VDC BIM Coordinator
description: **Description** At John Paul Construction, we believe every project is an opportunity to improve on what has come before. To foster that mindset, we hire the brightest and best in the industry, encourage collaboration, and embrace innovative thinking and new technology. Due to continued expansion, we are hiring a VDC BIM Coordinator for our various projects in Dublin and Head Office. **Key Responsibilities** * Take full responsibility for the BIM process on the project. * Implement and maintain the BIM Execution Plan. * Ensure all project team members adhere to the BIM Execution Plan. * Establish BIM coordination workshops and report and record progress. * Record BIM models and their status. * Establish quality control procedures to check all models are accurate and that the level of model definition is appropriate for each project stage. * Identify and document clashes between different models using Navisworks Manage, and chair clash detection meetings with other parties to the contract, including design consultants and sub\-contractors. * Advise members of the team of significant clashes and information issues associated with the BIM aspect of the works. * Record and monitor shared data and relationships between models (grids, floor levels, and shared coordinates). * Identify and agree shared technical requirements, software package interoperability, and BIM standards to be delivered by each team member. * Monitor the uploading of information models to the Common Data Environment, ensuring that they are compliant with the BIM Execution Plan. * Co\-ordinate handover of models and data at the agreed milestones. * Carry out model generation from time to time as necessary to facilitate the development of the design on the project. * Ensure that the level of skill and expertise is kept up to date in what is a very rapidly changing environment. * All other duties associated with this role. **Specific Requirements** * Third\-level qualification in Construction / Structural / Services. * Minimum 5 years’ post\-graduate experience in a related field. * Previous experience working for a main contractor is desirable. * Proficient in Microsoft Office (Excel, Word). * Proficient in Navisworks Manage. * Working knowledge of AutoCAD, Revit MEP, and Revit Architecture. * Knowledge and understanding of BIM procedures and protocols. * Excellent communication skills. * Ability to work on own initiative. **Package** As a permanent employee of John Paul Construction, you will benefit from: * Competitive salary * Relocation allowance (where applicable) * Company laptop and phone * Pension * Life insurance and income protection * Continuous Professional Development (CPD) * Access to lifestyle benefits such as the Bike to Work scheme, Employee Assistance Programme, Sports \& Social Club, and more We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We believe in fostering a work environment where all individuals are treated with dignity and respect, and are given the opportunity to reach their full potential.

title: Vice President, Credit Officer - Corporates Credit
description: **Job Description:** **Job Title:** Credit Officer **Corporate Title:** Vice President **Location:** London **Company Overview** At Bank of America, we are guided by a common purpose to help make financial lives better through the power of every connection. Responsible Growth is how we run our company and how we deliver for our clients, teammates, communities and shareholders every day. One of the keys to driving Responsible Growth is being a great place to work for our teammates around the world. We’re devoted to being a diverse and inclusive workplace for everyone. We hire individuals with a broad range of backgrounds and experiences and invest heavily in our teammates and their families by offering competitive benefits to support their physical, emotional, and financial well\-being. Bank of America believes both in the importance of working together and offering flexibility to our employees. We use a multi\-faceted approach for flexibility, depending on the various roles in our organization. Working at Bank of America will give you a great career with opportunities to learn, grow and make an impact, along with the power to make a difference. Join us! **London Location Overview** Our London office is based just a stone’s throw from the magnificent St. Paul’s Cathedral on bustling King Edward Street. Here you’ll find modern workspaces and a state\-of\-the\-art auditorium space. In addition, we’re proud to host an onsite restaurant that shares our commitment to sustainability by providing delicious seasonal menus which have been created with the planet in mind. Make sure to take time for yourself and head up to our rooftop terrace and take in the spectacular views across London. Finally, your physical wellness is well\-catered for with our onsite gym facilities and medical centre. **Job Description** An exciting opportunity for a Vice President to join our Corporates Credit team a part of Global Corporate and Investment Banking (GCIB) Credit managing and delivering credit relationships for corporate clients in accordance with the company’s growth strategy, risk appetite and underwriting standards. We are an independent function that works closely with various lines of business, relationship managers, product specialists and Risk. GCIB Credit is responsible for analysing and delivering credit underwriting debt financing solutions and derivative strategies for medium and large corporate clients of GCIB \& Global Markets (GM). GCIB includes corporate banking and investment banking and GM includes sales \& trading. The group provides services to middle\-market and large corporations, government entities and institutional investors. Services including mergers and acquisition advice, equity and debt capital raising, corporate lending, sales and trading in fixed income, commodities, currency and equities, risk management, research, and liquidity and payments management. GCIB Credit partners with teams across GCIB \& GM including Trade Service Partners and Global Treasury Management to drive business growth and results while maintaining appropriate risk discipline specific to the corporate credit portfolio. Integral to GCIB Credit’s mission is its partnership with other branches of GCIB, GM and Risk Management organisation in order to effectively manage and monitor the credit risks related to the corporate portfolio. **Responsibilities** * Perform financial and credit analysis, risk rating analysis ensuring adherence to prevailing credit policy and procedures for an assigned portfolio. * Independently assess credit worthiness, perform intensive qualitative and quantitative due diligence of prospective borrowers and/or trading counterparties and establish appropriate terms and conditions to mitigate risk. * Structuring transactions, underwriting and negotiating credit documentation designed to meet both client and investor needs including credit agreements, amendments, derivative documentation. * Ensuring granularity of the portfolio with specific reference to targets for net country/credit capital, legally binding commitments, total exposure and asset quality. * Establish appropriate internal risk ratings for clients and implement accurate adjustments throughout the client’s credit life cycle. * Ensuring that the portfolio and credit management process are at all times rated "satisfactory" by internal auditors, the Credit Review group and external Regulators. * Develop and maintain a deep understanding of the product spectrum and portfolio at the client, industry, and macro level to assess risk and craft value added solutions balancing risk. * Develop, maintain, and manage internal relationships with business partners including Corporate and Investment Bankers, Sales \& Trading, Risk Management, Syndications, and Legal. * Participate in strategic projects to support business goals. * Providing provide appropriate coaching and development to junior teammates. **What We Are Looking For** * Credit risk experience at a major international financial institution, analysing, assessing companies and knowledge of EMEA and specific industry sectors, structuring transactions and documentation. * Effective credit skills, risk judgment and broader risk awareness and understanding of the wider business, market and regulatory environment in which the bank operates, and how that impacts client and portfolio’s credit profile and ability to defend rigorously a fact\-based point of view. * BA/BSc degree (or equivalent); Finance, Accounting or Economics would be beneficial. * Sound product/technical, documentation and process knowledge across lending, trade finance and derivative (FX, rates, commodities and equities) products. * Acting as an agent of change/improvement, influencing corporate policy/procedure and projecting a positive strategic vision. * Commercial mindset to support value added discussions with effective interpersonal and written and verbal communication skills, and attention to detail and accuracy. * Fluency with use of Microsoft Office applications, particularly Excel; including ability to prepare and/or review company specific financial models in Excel. * Strong organisational skills with ability to prioritise and work under pressure, drive deal selection, negotiate solutions, strong transaction management process, and proactively manage credit risks. * Fluency in English required. Additionally, another European language skills highly beneficial. **Benefits Of Working At Bank Of America** **UK** * Private healthcare for you and your family plus an annual health screen to help you manage your physical wellness with the option to purchase a screen for your partner * Competitive pension plan, life assurance and group income protection cover if you become unable to work as a result of a disability or health reasons * 20 days of back\-up childcare including access to school holiday clubs and 20 days of back\-up adult care per annum * The ability to change your core benefits as well as the option of selecting a variety of flexible benefits to suit your personal circumstances including access to a wellbeing account, travel insurance, critical illness etc. * Access to an emotional wellbeing helpline, mental health first aiders and virtual GP services. * Access to an Employee Assistance Program for confidential support and help for everyday matters * Ability to donate to charities of your choice directly through payroll and the bank will match your contribution * Opportunity to access our Arts \& Culture corporate membership program and receive discounted entry to some of the UK’s most iconic cultural institutions and exhibitions. * Opportunity to give back to your community, develop new skills and work with new groups of people by volunteering in your local community. **Bank of America** Good conduct and sound judgment is crucial to our long term success. It’s important that all employees in the organisation understand the expected standards of conduct and how we manage conduct risk. Individual accountability and an ownership mind\-set are the cornerstones of our Code of Conduct and are at the heart of managing risk well. We are an equal opportunities employer and ensure that no applicant is subject to less favourable treatment on the grounds of gender, gender identity or gender reassignment, marital or civil partner status, race, religion or belief, colour, nationality, ethnic or national origins, membership of the Traveller community, age, sexual orientation, pregnancy or maternity, civil status, socio\-economic background, family status or physical or mental disability. The Bank selects candidates for interview based on their skills, qualifications and experience. We strive to ensure that our recruitment processes are accessible for all candidates and encourage any candidates to tell us about any adjustment requirements.

title: Vulnerability Analyst
description: Application Security Vulnerability Analyst **Location:** Limerick (Hybrid) **Contract:** 6‑month contract‑to‑hire **Industry:** Medical Devices / Regulated Software **Right to work:** Ireland/EU only (no sponsorship) Waters is seeking an **Application Security Vulnerability Analyst** to join its **Product Security team** in Limerick. This role focuses on **hands‑on vulnerability assessment of Windows‑based applications** used in regulated medical‑device products. This is **not a SOC, SIEM, cloud, or network security role** . The position is centred on **known vulnerabilities, exploitability analysis, and product impact assessment** . Responsibilities * Assess known software vulnerabilities (CVEs) impacting Windows‑based applications * Perform exploitability, impact, likelihood, and residual risk analysis * Analyse SBOMs and SCA outputs to understand third‑party component risk * Evaluate safety and patient risk in a regulated product environment * Produce clear vulnerability assessment and security risk documentation * Work closely with product security, engineering, and quality teams * Support internal, customer, and regulatory security queries Requirements **Essential** * 2\+ years' commercial experience in application security, vulnerability analysis, or vulnerability management * Strong understanding of CVEs, exploitability assessment, and risk scoring * Knowledge of secure software concepts (e.g. SQL injection, authentication flaws, privilege escalation) * Experience with Windows‑based applications and/or .NET environments * Strong written technical documentation skills **Desirable** * Experience with SCA and SBOM analysis * Exposure to regulated or safety‑critical software environments * Familiarity with standards such as IEC 81001‑5, ISO 27001, or NIST * Security certifications (Security\+, CEH, GIAC, etc.) Working model * Hybrid role based in Limerick * Onsite attendance required during onboarding and collaboration * Not fully remote Contract details * Initial 6‑month contract * Strong intention to convert to permanent employment (subject to approval) * Competitive hourly rate Eligibility * Must have **unrestricted right to work in Ireland (includes Brisith Citizens)** * **No visa sponsorship available** **Desired Skills and Experience** application security, vulnerability management, vulnerability analyst, CVE analysis, exploitability assessment, impact assessment, risk assessment, SBOM, SCA, software composition analysis, secure coding, secure software development, Windows applications, .NET, desktop software security, product security, AppSec engineer, vulnerability scanning, threat analysis, security reporting, risk documentation, medical device cybersecurity, regulated software, IEC 81001\-5, ISO 27001, NIST, authentication security, SQL injection, privilege escalation Security\+, CEH, CISSP, CISM, GIAC, GSEC, GPEN, GWAPT, OSCP, OSCE, CompTIA CySA\+, CompTIA Pentest\+, SSCP, CCSK, Certified DevSecOps Professional, ISO 27001 Lead title: Warehouse Operative (Goods In) - Newbridge
description: **Summary** From our Head Office to the shop floor and everywhere in between our primary goal is to create and deliver a fantastic customer journey for everyone who shops at Lidl. **Are you a warehouse operative looking for a challenge, high energy and love learning every day?** We are looking for Warehouse Operatives who are ambitious and match our values (performance, trust, respect, grounded and belonging). **What You'll Do** * Two different shifts available: Day shift; 6am\-4pm \& Night shift; 10pm\-6am * Maintain the uninterrupted flow of goods in and out of our regional distribution centre * Instructing the lorry drivers regarding use of lanes, and sorting the incoming stock * Quality inspection and quantitative counting of incoming stock (e.g. temperature measurement) * Recording all additional relevant data (e.g. best before date, pallet height) * Labelling every pallet with the number of the despatch unit * Always maintain health and safety standards **What You'll Need** * Comfortable with Manual Handling Equipment, such as an order picker forklift * A flexible can\-do attitude * Preferably, experience within a warehouse environment * A good team player * Comfortable working in a fast\-paced environment **What You'll Receive** We offer a competitive and transparent salary system, that ensures pay equality across all positions at Lidl * €15\.40 rising to €17\.90 per hour after 3 years (supplementary pay outlined below) * Unsocial hours worked (12am to 7am) * 20 days holidays per annum pro rata * Company pension * Genuine opportunities for career development * Individuals recognised as high performing will be invited to join our Leadership Academy as part of your on\-going development * Circle K and Private Health Insurance discounts available for all employees * Bike to Work Scheme * Mobile and broadband discounts with Three network * Maternity \& Paternity Leave top up, Marriage Leave, Employee Assistance Programme **Year 1** * Basic Rate €15\.40 * €19\.25 (Unsocial Hours) * €23\.10 (Overtime/Sundays) * €30\.80 (Bank Holiday) **Year 2** * Basic Rate €16\.20 * €20\.25 (Unsocial Hours) * €24\.30 (Overtime/Sundays) * €32\.40 (Bank Holiday) **Year 3** * Basic Rate €16\.90 * €21\.13 (Unsocial Hours) * €25\.35 (Overtime/Sundays) * €33\.80 (Bank Holiday) **Year 4** * Basic Rate €17\.90 * €22\.38 (Unsocial Hours) * €26\.85 (Overtime/Sundays) * €35\.80 (Bank Holiday) Lidl is an equal opportunities employer. If you have any particular requirements, we will ensure any reasonable accommodations are implemented as part of the recruitment and selection process. We value diversity and inclusion in our workplace and want to ensure that all applicants have the same opportunity regardless of gender, civil status, family status, sexual orientation, age, disability, race, religious belief, political opinion or membership of the traveller community.
